items.title: CLP, Labor have 'blood on their hands' after remote death, MLA says

children.content: An independent member of the Northern Territory parliament says successive NT governments "have blood on their hands", following the death of a 26-year-old man in a remote Arnhem Land community this week.

gallery.altText: A photo of an Indigenous man in a suit, speaking outside NT Parliament.

thumbnail.altText: A photo of an Indigenous man in a suit, speaking outside NT Parliament.

items.title: Woman suffers brain injury after 'toxic dose' of wrong drug

children.content: A 36-year-old woman who says she suffered a "hypoxic brain injury" when she was given a "toxic dose" of the wrong drug during routine surgery at Palmerston Regional Hospital is suing the Northern Territory government for negligence.

image.altText: A patient laying on a hospital bed, knees up with blanket covering their legs, holding a cup next to urine sample container

items.title: Major upgrades planned for 200-hectare site once used as TV, film backdrop

children.content: Australian-based global tour operator Intrepid has announced a multi-million-dollar investment in Central Australia's Ooraminna Homestead, 30 kilometres south of Alice Springs.

image.altText: A wide angle shot of a small tin cabin nestled among trees in a rocky outback landscape.

items.title: Australian bush tea hits shelves in Asia

children.content: The product is being sent from a remote property in the Northern Territory all the way to Japan, Korea and China.

gallery.altText: A close up image of tea leaves in a pot.

thumbnail.altText: A close up image of tea leaves in a pot.

items.title: 26yo man dead after violent disturbance in remote NT community

children.content: A man has died after a violent disturbance in Milingimbi yesterday, in which police allege some people were armed with edged weapons.

image.altText: A bird's eye view of Milingimbi

items.title: Aboriginal affairs plan launched as NT goes backwards on Closing the Gap targets

children.content: The Northern Territory government has released its Aboriginal Affairs framework as the territory goes backwards on several Closing the Gap targets.

image.altText: Steve Edgington wears a suit and tie while standing outside NT Parliament.

items.title: Report finds Darwin Harbour biodiversity rivals that of Ningaloo Reef

children.content: New research has revealed Darwin Harbour's biodiversity, including its wide range of rare species, rivals that of the World Heritage-protected Ningaloo Reef.

items.title: Jury hears 'no bad blood' before sistergirl's alleged fatal stabbing

children.content: Jurors in the murder trial of a man accused of fatally stabbing a 37-year-old sistergirl from the Tiwi Islands have heard there was "no bad blood" between them in the lead-up to the "family tragedy".

image.altText: A First Nations man wearing mirrored sunglasses, pointing at himself.

items.title: School teacher who broke student's teeth found guilty of causing serious harm

children.content: A former Darwin Middle School teacher who struck a female student in the face, breaking her teeth, after she demanded her confiscated phone back has been found guilty by a jury of unlawfully causing serious harm.

image.altText: A man in a shirt and tie leaves court

items.title: Young people leaving Sydney 'en mass' over housing affordability

children.content: Productivity Commissioner Danielle Wood says Australia will not meet the federal government's 1.2 million new home target, as residential construction rates fall.

image.altText: People walk through the front door of a house, past a For Lease sign

items.title: Europe was a word British PMs dared not say. Then Donald Trump returned

children.content: Keir Starmer was never a fan of Brexit. Now, under the cover of the war in Iran and a fractured US relationship, he's looking to Europe to return Britain to the fold.

image.altText: Keir Starmer speaks in the House of Commons

items.title: Chinese device found in strategic waters near Bali identified

children.content: A large torpedo-shaped device discovered near an island in a strategically important strait between Bali and Lombok has been identified by defence analysts as a Chinese undersea monitoring system.

children.content: The Royal Bank of Canada (RBC) expects Australian inflation to surge to 6 per cent, while adding another RBA interest rate hike to its forecast.

items.title: 'Australians deserve to know': Victorian political donations site goes dark

children.content: Victoria has been left without laws to govern party donation disclosures — exposing the state's politics to a flood of unchecked donations.

image.altText: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan touches her glasses while at a press conference.

items.title: Mother of 11 who 'never thought we'd be homeless' pleads for help

children.content: Finding a home for a large family in a regional area has left one family camping on the side of the highway in rural New South Wales. Nationally, Indigenous people are over-represented in homelessness statistics.

items.title: St Kilda announces appeal against Collard ban for homophobic slur

items.title: Elderly couple beg council to let them stay in Gold Coast caravan park

children.content: Time is running out for residents facing eviction from Kirra Beach Tourist Park. In 2018, the council told them they needed to move out by 2028 to make way for tourists, but with rising rent and property prices, they say they cannot afford to live anywhere else.

items.title: Former Arsenal, Juventus goalkeeper killed after car hit by train
